name: Viaje al Otro Mundo — circular desde La Fresneda por la Comarca del Matarraña (Ruta 4)
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Fresneda offers routes through the Comarca del Matarraña, a region characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and extensive olive and almond groves. The landscape features a mix of agricultural land and Mediterranean forests, providing varied terrain for mountain bikers. Elevations are generally moderate, with some routes including more significant climbs. The area's geology includes limestone formations, contributing to diverse trail conditions. Best mountain bike trails around La Fresneda The most popular mountain bike trail is Viaje al Otro Mundo — circular desde La Fresneda por la Comarca del Matarraña (Ruta 4), a 34.5 miles (55.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores varied terrain and offers expansive views across the Matarraña region. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Valderrobres Stone Bridge – Riverside of Valderrobres loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda, a moderate 23.0 miles (37.0 km) path. This route follows river paths and passes historical structures like the Valderrobres Stone Bridge. Local mountain bikers also love the Riverside of Valderrobres – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda, a 22.7 miles (36.6 km) trail leading through river valleys and past the historic Valderrobres Stone Bridge, often completed in about 3 hours 0 minutes. Mountain biking around La Fresneda is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in La Fresneda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Fresneda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around La Fresneda?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around La Fresneda, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 5 easy routes, 41 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in La Fresneda?
A: Yes, La Fresneda offers several easy mountain bike trails. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside of Valderrobres – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from Antigua Estación de Torre del Compte&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.1 miles (25.9 km) long and takes about 2 hours to complete, providing a gentle introduction to the region's landscapes.

Q: What options are available for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Experienced mountain bikers can find challenging routes in La Fresneda. The region features 14 difficult trails, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995902028/viaje-al-otro-mundo-circular-desde-la-fresneda-por-la-comarca-del-matarrana-ruta-4" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaje al Otro Mundo — circular desde La Fresneda por la Comarca del Matarraña (Ruta 4)&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 34.5 miles (55.6 km) long with significant elevation changes, exploring varied terrain and offering expansive views across the Matarraña region.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike routes in La Fresneda?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the area's 5 easy routes and many moderate trails can be suitable for families depending on their experience. These routes often follow river paths or agricultural tracks, providing a less strenuous ride through the scenic Comarca del Matarraña.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of mountain bike trails around La Fresneda?
A: The mountain bike trails around La Fresneda vary significantly in length and duration. Many routes range from 15 to 25 miles (24 to 40 km) and can take between 2.5 to 4 hours to complete. Longer, more challenging routes, like the 34.5-mile (55.6 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995902028/viaje-al-otro-mundo-circular-desde-la-fresneda-por-la-comarca-del-matarrana-ruta-4" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaje al Otro Mundo&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 5 hours.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available from La Fresneda?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around La Fresneda are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valderrobres Stone Bridge – Riverside of Valderrobres loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31492694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside of Valderrobres – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic loops through river valleys.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Several trails pass by notable landmarks. For instance, routes often follow river paths and pass historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valderrobres Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Other attractions in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Compte Bell Tower&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2752464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Equinox Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;. For natural sights, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Pena&lt;/a&gt; lake or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in La Fresneda?
A: The best time for mountain biking in La Fresneda is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions through the olive groves and Mediterranean forests. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any trails with good viewpoints in the La Fresneda area?
A: Yes, the rolling hills and varied terrain of the Matarraña region offer several opportunities for scenic viewpoints. Many routes provide expansive views across the agricultural landscapes and river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7406207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caixa del Pantano&lt;/a&gt; is a specific highlight known for its views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in La Fresneda?
A: The mountain bike trails around La Fresneda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the Comarca del Matarraña, and the mix of agricultural land and Mediterranean forests that provide varied and engaging routes.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking on trails around La Fresneda?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on the established trails around La Fresneda. However, it's always advisable to respect private property, stay on marked paths, and be aware of any local regulations, especially when passing through agricultural areas or protected zones.

Q: Where can I find parking near the mountain bike trailheads in La Fresneda?
A: While specific parking areas aren't detailed for every trailhead, La Fresneda, being a small town, typically offers public parking in or near its main square or at the periphery of the village. Many routes start directly from the town, making it convenient to park centrally and begin your ride.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to refuel along the mountain bike routes?
A: La Fresneda itself has cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. Many routes pass through or near other small villages in the Comarca del Matarraña, such as Valderrobres, which also offer amenities like cafes and shops. It's a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes, as services may be sparse between villages.
